data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate plan-review + completion-verification onto the workflow engine) × THIS child B1 (723c: Production batch-runner — extract sizing.py behind epic A's BatchRunner seam)]

Walked all 7 cross-child interaction modes for B1 against the parent + the WHOLE sibling roster (0408, 101a/B4, 5506/B-DM, 6b06/B2, 7e48/B6, 92fa/B-UX, 961b/B-RETIRE, dc58/B3, f12d/B5). NO high-confidence findings — CLEAN pairing. Per-mode benign rationale:

(1) Implicit shared state — CLEAN. B1 part-1 extracts the Pass-1 finder machinery from orchestrator.py into a NEW plan_review/pass1.py (run_pass1 + material_fingerprint + CONTAINER_CRITERIA helpers), re-exporting material_fingerprint for attest.py/__init__.py. This touches the live claim gate, but was verified byte-for-byte behaviour-preserving (106 plan-review tests identical green before/after; code-review subagent confirmed zero-diff run_pass1 body, orchestrator 743→491 LOC). The extracted run_pass1 is then SHARED by the bespoke path (orchestrator._run_passes) and the workflow path (ProductionBatchRunner) — and B-RETIRE (961b) explicitly KEEPS pass1.run_pass1 / sizing / ProductionBatchRunner alive when it removes bespoke run_review. Shared surface is acknowledged and sequenced across siblings, not hidden concurrent mutation; no write-write race / stale-read.

(2) Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN. B1's seam design (D3, batch-runner-seam.md) resolves the AgentStepRunner↔rebar.llm.Runner impedance mismatch: the production runner OWNS an injectable rebar.llm.Runner and the seam's agent_runner is intentionally UNUSED. Every consuming sibling shares this assumption — B2 (6b06) drives ProductionBatchRunner(runner=finder); B4 (101a) captures at the TracingFakeRunner (rebar.llm.Runner) seam for finders; B5 (f12d) dispatches verdict production via that runner. The opaque-plan / interpreter-never-branches invariant B1 journals is the same epic-A invariant B2's interpreter relies on. Consistent.

(3) Dependency gap — CLEAN. B1 depends on epic A's BatchRunner/BatchRunRequest/BatchRunResult seam (cross-epic dep on 5766 at the parent) and produces the runner B2 consumes — dep 723c blocks 6b06 is declared. No missing producer/consumer link in the roster.

(4) Scope overlap — CLEAN. AC3 pins sizing.py as the SINGLE home of the sizing algorithm (no duplication); the new module is a thin adapter (verified: production_batch_runner.py carries no ladder/budget/shed algorithm — all flows through run_pass1 → sizing.*). B2 owns the workflow YAML authoring; B4 reads the runner's trace; B5 layers cutover. Distinct layers over B1's artifact, not duplicated ownership. The catalog-lint de-risk note is a B2/B-DM surface, not B1's (B1's batch step uses plan-review-finder as system prompt and resolves criterion descriptors via registry.by_id()).

(5) Ordering violation — CLEAN. Pipeline B1 → B2 → B4 → B5 → B-RETIRE matches declared deps (723c blocks 6b06; 6b06 blocks 101a; 101a blocks f12d; f12d blocks 961b). B1's internal sequencing (part-1 behaviour-preserving extraction landed BEFORE part-2 runner, isolating the risky live-gate change) is sound.

(6) Consumer impact — CLEAN. B1 is explicitly additive/offline ("No change to the bespoke orchestrator's behaviour yet (cutover is B5); this only makes the production runner available behind the seam"). The pass1.py extraction is behaviour-preserving, so the live claim gate is untouched at B1's boundary. The live cutover (and any consumer-facing change) is a B5 artifact.

(7) Residual references — CLEAN. B1 references sizing.py, registry.py, det_floor/passes (via run_pass1), and the epic-A seam — all of which B-RETIRE (961b) explicitly PRESERVES. B6 (7e48) removes only examples/code_review.yaml + templates.py, which B1 does not touch. The material_fingerprint re-export keeps attest.py/__init__.py consumers wired.

ANTI-FP note: B1 is a thin, offline, behaviour-preserving adapter whose seam contract, ownership boundary, dependency links, and ordering are all consistent with — and explicitly referenced by — its consuming siblings (B2/B4/B5) and its downstream retirement story (B-RETIRE). The known epic-level live-plumbing defect (the {{plan}} variable gap surfaced on B2/0408/B5) is OFF B1's boundary: B1 supplies only the FINDER batch, whose prompt variables come from passes.pass1_chunk, not the verify/coach steps that lack {{plan}}. No high-confidence cross-child inconsistency attributable to this pairing. Findings list: EMPTY.
